cards.front: How to declare a one-dimensional array in C++
What is the purpose of the #include statement in C++
cards.back: to include the necessary libraries and headers into the program

cards.front: What is the difference between a C-style array and a reference to an array
cards.back: A C-style array is a separate object, while a reference to an array is a pointer to the array

cards.front: How to initialize a two-dimensional array in C++
What is the purpose of the nullptr keyword in C++
cards.back: to represent a null pointer, indicating that the pointer is not pointing to a valid memory location
